name: Orange County Fair Housing Council, Inc
description_short: Non-profit fair housing council providing free education, counseling, and enforcement services to combat housing discrimination in Orange County since 1965.
description_long:
The Fair Housing Council of Orange County (FHCOC) was founded in 1965 by Richard Petherbridge, a Santa Ana attorney and ACLU advocate, in response to widespread racial discrimination in housing and California's Proposition 14. The organization incorporated in 1968, the same year Congress passed the Fair Housing Act. Operating as a member of the National Fair Housing Alliance with a volunteer board of directors and paid staff, FHCOC has served Orange County for nearly 60 years with a core mission to protect quality of life by ensuring equal access to housing and fostering diversity.

FHCOC offers comprehensive services including fair housing enforcement and discrimination investigation, HUD-approved housing counseling, landlord-tenant mediation, community education programs, and low-cost advocacy services. They provide free consultations for individuals experiencing housing discrimination and conduct surveys to document discrimination patterns. The organization operates from their Santa Ana office and is accessible via phone, email, and website.

What distinguishes FHCOC is its deep historical roots in civil rights advocacy combined with formal HUD certification for counseling services. The organization has maintained continuity of mission and leadership over decades, with founding board member Robert Johnson serving for nearly five decades. As an NFCC-affiliated entity offering HUD-certified counseling, they provide government-endorsed services at no cost to consumers.

However, FHCOC is fundamentally a fair housing advocacy and discrimination-fighting organization, not a consumer credit or financial counseling service in the traditional sense. While they likely address housing affordability and landlord-tenant financial disputes, their primary focus is enforcing fair housing laws rather than providing credit repair, debt management, or personal financial counseling. Consumers seeking traditional credit counseling may need to explore their full service offerings or seek referrals to complementary NFCC members.
pros: Free consultations for housing discrimination cases with no upfront fees
HUD-approved counseling services with NFCC affiliation and certified counselors
Established 1965 organization with nearly 60 years of fair housing expertise and credibility
Multiple contact methods: phone (714.569-0823, 1-800.698.FAIR), email, and in-person at Santa Ana office
Comprehensive services including enforcement, mediation, education, and low-cost advocacy
Operating member of National Fair Housing Alliance with volunteer board leadership
Document discrimination through formal housing discrimination surveys
cons: Limited to Orange County service area only despite national fair housing mission
Website lacks clear pricing, service timelines, or detailed service descriptions beyond brief menu items
No online intake forms or application process visible; requires phone/email contact to initiate services
Organization focused on fair housing enforcement rather than traditional credit counseling or financial management
Minimal information about complaint resolution success rates or case outcomes on public website
best_for: Orange County renters or homebuyers experiencing housing discrimination based on protected class
Individuals in landlord-tenant disputes seeking free mediation services
Consumers needing HUD-certified housing counseling for affordability or fair lending issues
Community organizations seeking fair housing education programs and training
diagnosis: FHCOC is best for Orange County residents facing housing discrimination or needing fair housing education and HUD-certified counseling at no cost. The primary caveat is that this organization's mission centers on fair housing enforcement and discrimination prevention rather than credit repair, debt management, or general financial counseling—consumers seeking traditional consumer credit services should clarify whether their specific needs fall within FHCOC's scope before contacting.
services: Fair housing enforcement and discrimination investigation
Community education programs on fair housing rights
Free consultations for housing discrimination complaints
Individual counseling on fair housing issues
Referrals and partnerships through National Fair Housing Alliance
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2021 E 4Th St, Suite 122, Santa Ana, CA 92705
